comment: Quality Gates catalog -- subjective dimension (cross-dimension per quality/catalogs/README.md). Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. Verifier impl: .claude/skills/reposix-quality-review/. Reports: quality/reports/verifications/subjective/. P61 Wave A catalog-first commit -- 3 rubric rows + short-lived waivers tracked_in P61 Wave G.

asserts: the doc-clarity-review subagent verdict for README.md hero (lines 1-50) is CLEAR (numeric score >= 7 on the 10-pt scale where CLEAR=10, NEEDS-WORK=5, CONFUSING=2)
the doc-clarity-review subagent verdict for docs/index.md hero (lines 1-50) is CLEAR (numeric score >= 7)
the verdict artifact at quality/reports/verifications/subjective/cold-reader-hero-clarity.json contains {ts, score, verdict, rationale, evidence_files} per the dimension README JSON shape

rows.owner_hint: if RED: README.md hero or docs/index.md hero confused the cold reader -- read the rubric artifact at quality/reports/verifications/subjective/cold-reader-hero-clarity.json for the friction points + rewrite the offending paragraphs. Common: jargon-heavy first paragraph; missing pitch; install command buried.

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04), NOT mooted by 90-03's dvcs-cold-reader dispatch wiring (D90-02b) -- this waiver covers a different gap: the runner subprocess lacks Task tool access, so a bare re-sweep would overwrite the ratified Path B artifact (score 8 CLEAR) with a Path-B stub (`dispatch_inline_subagent.sh` persists --score 0 --verdict CONFUSING + exit 1 when `claude` CLI is absent). The dispatch-and-preserve invariant fix (MIGRATE-03 shape) still does not exist in the runner. The 'v0.12.1 MIGRATE-03' label was dead -- repointed per D90-02(c) mass-renewal rule.
waiver.tracked_in: P95 (Quality framework upgrade + doc fixes) -- runner dispatch-and-preserve invariant for subjective-graded rows

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If README.md or docs/index.md hero copy actually confused a cold reader, the dispatched doc-clarity-review subagent would score below 7/10 CLEAR on at least one surface, and that sub-7 score is recorded directly in the ratified artifact -- the score IS the falsifying evidence, not a proxy for it.

sources: CLAUDE.md:Freshness invariants -- Install path leads with package manager

asserts: the rubric subagent confirms README.md hero install snippet leads with `cargo binstall` OR `brew install` OR `cargo install` (NOT `git clone && cargo build`) per CLAUDE.md freshness-invariants
the rubric subagent confirms docs/index.md hero install snippet leads with the same package-manager-first ordering

numeric score >= 7 on a 1-10 scale where 10 = leads with package manager + cites supported targets + names the binstall fallback path; 5 = leads with package manager but missing target list; 2 = leads with git-clone+build
the verdict artifact at quality/reports/verifications/subjective/install-positioning.json contains {ts, score, verdict, rationale, evidence_files} per the dimension README JSON shape

rows.owner_hint: if RED: README.md or docs/index.md install copy regressed (perhaps a refactor moved the package-manager command below a git-clone snippet). Restore package-manager-first ordering per CLAUDE.md freshness invariants. Cross-check with structure/install-path-leads-with-pm in freshness-invariants.json.

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04), NOT mooted by 90-03's dvcs-cold-reader dispatch wiring (D90-02b) -- same runner dispatch-and-preserve gap as cold-reader-hero-clarity: no Task tool access in the runner subprocess means a bare re-sweep would clobber the ratified Path B artifact (score 9 CLEAR) with a stub. The 'v0.12.1 MIGRATE-03' label was dead -- repointed per D90-02(c) mass-renewal rule.
waiver.tracked_in: P95 (Quality framework upgrade + doc fixes) -- runner dispatch-and-preserve invariant for subjective-graded rows

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the install snippet actually led with git-clone+build instead of a package manager, the rubric subagent would score below 7/10 on the package-manager-first criterion and name the offending line in its rationale -- the recorded score+rationale directly falsifies (or confirms) the claim, not a proxy signal.

asserts: the rubric subagent cross-checks every headline number in README.md + docs/index.md against the source-of-truth artifacts in benchmarks/ + docs/benchmarks/ -- and confirms drift is <= 2pp for percentage claims OR within stated confidence interval for absolute numbers

numeric score >= 7 on a 1-10 scale where 10 = every headline number cites a benchmarks/ artifact and value is current; 5 = some numbers cite source but are stale (3-5pp drift); 2 = numbers stand alone with no source citation

the verdict artifact at quality/reports/verifications/subjective/headline-numbers-sanity.json contains {ts, score, verdict, rationale, evidence_files} + a per-headline-number table (number | source-file:line | source-of-truth-file | drift)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a headline number drifted from the benchmarks/ source-of-truth (typical: a perf optimization landed and bench output updated, but the README/docs hero copy was not synced). Update the README + docs hero numbers OR refresh the benchmarks artifact (whichever is the actual ground truth).

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04), NOT mooted by 90-03's dvcs-cold-reader dispatch wiring (D90-02b) -- same runner dispatch-and-preserve gap as the other two subjective rows: no Task tool access in the runner subprocess means a bare re-sweep would clobber the ratified Path B artifact (score 9 CLEAR) with a stub. The 'v0.12.1 MIGRATE-03' label was dead -- repointed per D90-02(c) mass-renewal rule.
waiver.tracked_in: P95 (Quality framework upgrade + doc fixes) -- runner dispatch-and-preserve invariant for subjective-graded rows

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If a headline number actually drifted from its benchmarks/ source, the rubric subagent's per-headline-number table would show a drift value exceeding the stated threshold and the numeric score would drop below 7 -- the table entry is the falsifying evidence, not an inference from it.

asserts: the doc-clarity-review subagent verdict for docs/concepts/dvcs-topology.md is CLEAR (numeric score >= 7 on the 10-pt scale where CLEAR=10, NEEDS-WORK=5, CONFUSING=2) when graded against a reader profile who has read only docs/index.md + docs/concepts/mental-model-in-60-seconds.md

the doc-clarity-review subagent verdict for docs/guides/dvcs-mirror-setup.md is CLEAR (numeric score >= 7) — the cold reader can complete the walk-through end-to-end (Steps 1-5 + cleanup) without needing to consult external docs beyond the linked Atlassian webhook docs

the doc-clarity-review subagent verdict for the new 'DVCS push/pull issues' section in docs/guides/troubleshooting.md is CLEAR (numeric score >= 7) — each of the four entries (bus fetch-first, attach reconciliation, webhook race, cache-desync) names the symptom + the recovery move in concrete shell commands

zero critical-friction findings (per CLAUDE.md OP-1 / 'Cold-reader pass on user-facing surfaces'); non-critical findings either fixed in-phase or filed to .planning/milestones/v0.13.0-phases/GOOD-TO-HAVES.md for P88
the verdict artifact at quality/reports/verifications/subjective/dvcs-cold-reader.json contains {ts, score, verdict, rationale, evidence_files} per the dimension README JSON shape

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs/concepts/dvcs-topology.md or docs/guides/dvcs-mirror-setup.md confused the cold reader (one who has read only docs/index.md + docs/concepts/mental-model-in-60-seconds.md). Read the rubric artifact at quality/reports/verifications/subjective/dvcs-cold-reader.json for the friction points + rewrite the offending paragraphs. Common: jargon-above-Layer-3 leaks (FUSE/partial-clone/promisor/stateless-connect/fast-import/protocol-v2 — banned per scripts/banned-words-lint.sh in concepts + guides); diagram unclear; mirror-lag refs vs current-SoT-state confusion (Q2.2); steps in mirror-setup.md missing prerequisite. Owner runs `/reposix-quality-review --rubric dvcs-cold-reader` post-phase to flip from NOT-VERIFIED to PASS.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the DVCS docs were actually confusing to a cold reader, the dispatched doc-clarity-review subagent would score below the 7/10 CLEAR threshold on at least one of the three graded surfaces, or would surface a critical-friction finding -- either directly falsifies the 'clear to a cold reader' claim this row exists to police and the verdict artifact records the sub-7 score / finding as the falsifying evidence.

rubric.reader_profile: Has read only docs/index.md + docs/concepts/mental-model-in-60-seconds.md; never seen v0.13.0 docs; understands single-backend reposix but not DVCS topology; goal: install the mirror sync against a confluence space without escalating to a maintainer.
criteria: Three roles (SoT-holder, mirror-only consumer, round-tripper) are introduced before any of them is used in a sentence
The mirror-lag refs explanation does not let the reader confuse 'last synced' with 'current SoT state' (Q2.2)
The when-to-choose-which-pattern guidance gives the reader a clear self-routing path
The mirror-setup walk-through is runnable as-written: every gh/curl/sed command works without modification when the reader substitutes their org/space
The cleanup procedure is complete enough that the reader can tear down without leaving orphan refs/secrets/webhooks
Troubleshooting entries name the symptom in concrete stderr text and the recovery in concrete shell commands
